title: mentha sylvestris var. alopecuroides
intro: Mentha sylvestris var. alopecuroides, also known as field balm or wild balm, is a variety of the wild mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Mentha sylvestris var. alopecuroides include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and provide relief from headaches and fever. This plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
